methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $39.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $14.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

private.name: Luma AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Runway
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Magic AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B+

data.narrative:
Figure AI, a Series C AI & Software company founded by Brett Adcock, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) including Skild AI, Luma AI, Runway, and Magic AI at the same stage. The company's last post-money valuation stands at $39.0B on $1B+ raised.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression, using a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier against the $39.0B base, implies a valuation of $66.3B. Peer-Median across 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $2.8B, with a range of $1.5B to $14.0B (Skild AI at the high end). The substantial divergence between methods reflects material heterogeneity within the AI & Software frontier cohort and the outsized position of Figure AI relative to its peer set.

Triangulated analysis supports a range of $2.3B to $76.2B, with a median of $34.5B. The wide band reflects tension between stage-typical progression dynamics and peer-relative positioning. Key uncertainties include whether Figure AI's current $39.0B valuation reflects genuine differentiation in robotics or applications versus peer companies, and whether the 1.7x multiplier appropriately calibrates Series C step progression in a frontier tech market. The peer-median method suggests substantial current overvaluation; stage-progression supports the existing valuation with upside. Resolution requires clarity on revenue, user growth, and deployment velocity relative to named comparables.
